title: fix: h100-mfu-calculation
body: The MFU calculation in the GPT model currently references a fixed A100 BF16 peak (312e12). This PR aims to make MFU GPU-specific (e.g., H100) by parameterizing the MFU peak and allowing selection between A100, H100, or a custom peak. The change targets the MFU estimation path used during training/logging to ensure MFU metrics reflect the chosen hardware, avoiding misleading measurements (e.g., 104% MFU on H100 when using A100 baselines).

step_plan.summary: Introduce a hardware-aware MFU peak parameter (mfu_peak_bf16) and propagate it into the GPT MFU estimator so MFU reflects the chosen GPU (A100/H100/custom) while preserving backward compatibility by default.

steps.operation: Store mfu_peak_bf16 from config in GPT.__init__ with a safe default
steps.reason: To enable hardware-aware MFU peak values and preserve backward compatibility by defaulting to 312e12 when config doesn't specify a value.
steps.side_effects: Adds a new instance attribute; downstream code may reference self.config.mfu_peak_bf16; no external API changes; unit tests should account for default value.

steps.operation: Refactor MFU estimation to use configurable peak
steps.reason: Replace hardcoded A100 BF16 peak with self.config.mfu_peak_bf16 to support H100 or custom peaks while preserving existing computation structure.
steps.side_effects: MFU results reflect configured peak; may affect MFU-derived metrics in logs; tests may need updates; ensures thread-safety if config is mutable; default preserves current behavior.
